10	/-!
11	# Free-presheaf Čech complex (P3b bridge — free-complex side)
12	
13	This file is the free-presheaf-complex side of the P3b bridge.  The section side lives in
14	`PresheafCech.lean`; this file owns the two free-complex declarations:
15	
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex`  (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`)
17	  — the chain complex of free presheaves of `O_X`-modules whose degree-`p` term is
18	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj`
19	  `  (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`.
20	
21	- `AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`  (`lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`)
22	  — the augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug : K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism / the free
23	  complex is a free resolution of `O_𝒰`.  **(built and axiom-clean.)**  The proof reduces objectwise
24	  (`quasiIso_of_evaluation`) to: the empty case `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty`; and the nonempty
25	  case `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty`, which transfers the engine quasi-isomorphism
26	  `cechEngineComplexAug_quasiIso` across `cechFreeEvalEngineIso`, using the degree-`0` augmentation
27	  identification `cechFreeAug_eval_eq` and the geometric identification
28	  `coverStructurePresheafEval_iso`.
29	
30	The quasi-isomorphism is proved objectwise (`quasiIso_of_evaluation`): for each open `V`,
31	the evaluated augmented complex is the augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full
32	simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with constant coefficients `O_X(V)`, which is
33	contractible when `I₁(V) ≠ ∅` (via the prepend-`i_fix` homotopy
34	`FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy`) and zero when `I₁(V) = ∅`.  This file owns the
35	combinatorial engine (`FreeCechEngine`) supplying that contracting homotopy and the
36	per-`V` sectionwise reductions building toward `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`.
37	-/
38	
39	universe u
40	
41	open CategoryTheory Limits
42	
43	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
44	
45	/-
46	Planner strategy (P3b free-complex side; see analogies/p3b-presheafcech.md +
47	blueprint §Presheaf-level Čech machinery):
48	
49	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50	`cechFreePresheafComplex`
51	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52	Goal: a `ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ` whose degree-`p` term is
53	  `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`
54	with the alternating-face differential.
55	
56	Recommended build path — use the simplicial route so that d²=0 comes for free:
57	1. Construct a `SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules` whose `n`-simplices are the
58	   above direct sums.
59	2. Apply `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` to obtain the chain complex.
60	   This gives d²=0 automatically via `SimplicialObject.boundarySquareZero`.
61	
62	Key API:
63	- `PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj :`
64	  `  ((Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u) ⥤ PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.obj`
65	  — the free-presheaf-of-modules functor.  Use it as `(PresheafOfModules.free _).obj` and
66	  `(PresheafOfModules.free _).map`.
67	- `yoneda.obj V : (Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u` — the representable presheaf of sets at `V`.
68	  Do NOT introduce a bespoke `j_!`; `free ∘ yoneda` is the correct substitute.
69	- `AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex` — turns a simplicial abelian group (or
70	  simplicial object in an abelian category) into a chain complex; d²=0 is a theorem.
71	- Direct sums: `⨁` is `DirectSum`; in `PresheafOfModules` use
72	  `Limits.biproduct` / `DirectSum.lof` / `DirectSum.desc`.
73	
74	DEAD END — do NOT hand-roll the alternating-sum identity for d²=0.  Use the simplicial
75	path above.
76	
77	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
78	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`
79	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	Goal: show that `cechFreePresheafComplex` → `O_𝒰[0]` is a quasi-isomorphism (i.e.,
81	the free complex is a free resolution of the structure sheaf restricted to the cover).
82	
83	Recommended build path — objectwise contracting homotopy:
84	1. Homology in `X.PresheafOfModules` is computed objectwise (colimits are objectwise).
85	   So it suffices to exhibit, for each open `V : Opens ↥X`, a contracting homotopy on
86	   the sectionwise complex `cechFreePresheafComplex(V)`.
87	2. Sectionwise at `V`, degree `p` is `⨁_{σ : Fin(p+1) → ι} R(V)` for those multi-indices
88	   `σ` with `V ⊆ ⨅ k, U (σ k)`, and `0` otherwise.
89	3. Fix any `i_fix : ι` with `V ⊆ U i_fix` (if none exists, the complex is `0`).
90	   The homotopy `h : K_p(V) → K_{p+1}(V)` maps the `σ`-summand to the `(i_fix, σ)`-summand
91	   (prepend `i_fix`).  Check `dh + hd = id` at each degree.
92	4. Package as `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy`, then use `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso`.
93	
94	Key API:
95	- `HomologicalComplex.Homotopy` — `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.Homotopy`.
96	- `HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso` (or `Homotopy.toQuasiIso`) — homotopy equivalence ⟹ quasi-iso.
97	
98	DEAD END — do NOT route through `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy`.  That
99	interface has a different index convention and is not directly applicable here.
100	-/
101	
102	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — free-presheaf Čech complex
103	
104	This section builds the free-presheaf Čech complex `cechFreePresheafComplex` of a finite
105	open cover `𝒰 : X.OpenCover` as the alternating-face-map chain complex of an explicit
106	simplicial object `cechFreeSimplicial`.  Everything is project-local: Mathlib has the
107	free-presheaf-of-modules functor, the Yoneda embedding and `alternatingFaceMapComplex`,
108	but not their assembly into the Čech free resolution of a cover.
109	
110	We require `[Finite 𝒰.I₀]` so that the index types `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` of the degreewise
111	coproducts are finite (`HasCoproductsOfShape`); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of
112	the downstream protected theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`. -/
113	
114	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
115	
116	/-- **Free presheaf of modules on a representable open.**
117	
118	The composite `Opens X --yoneda--> ((Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type) --free--> X.PresheafOfModules`.
119	For an open `V`, `freeYoneda.obj V` is the extension-by-zero free presheaf of modules
120	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
121	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
122	
123	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
124	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
125	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
126	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
127	
128	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
129	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
130	
131	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
132	
133	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
134	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
135	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
136	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
137	
138	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
139	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
140	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
141	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
142	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
143	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
144	
145	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
146	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
147	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
148	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
149	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
150	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
151	  subst e; simp
152	
153	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
154	
155	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
156	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
157	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
158	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
159	
160	Taking its `alternatingFaceMapComplex` produces `cechFreePresheafComplex` with `d² = 0` for
161	free (the simplicial route avoids hand-rolling the alternating-sum identity).
162	
163	Project-local: this is the simplicial backbone of the free Čech resolution; it has no
164	Mathlib counterpart. -/
165	noncomputable def cechFreeSimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
166	    SimplicialObject X.PresheafOfModules where
167	  obj n := ∐ fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
168	  map {n m} α := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
169	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 α.unop.toOrderHom σ)) ≫
170	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (m.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))
171	        (σ ∘ α.unop.toOrderHom)
172	  map_id n := by
173	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
174	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id]
175	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) = σ := by funext i; simp
176	    have eo : coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop))
177	        = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by rw [e]
178	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE
179	        (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 (SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (𝟙 n).unop) σ)) (eqToHom eo.symm),
180	      eqToHom_map]
181	    exact sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
182	      (fun σ : Fin (n.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) e
183	  map_comp {n m k} f g := by
184	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
185	    simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
186	    have e : σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom (f ≫ g).unop)
187	        = (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f.unop))
188	            ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom g.unop) := by
189	      funext i; simp [unop_comp, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom]
190	    rw [← sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport
191	          (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e,
192	      show (eqToHom (congrArg (fun τ : Fin (k.unop.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
193	              freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) e))
194	          = freeYoneda.map (eqToHom (congrArg (coverInterOpen 𝒰) e)) from (eqToHom_map _ _).symm]
195	    simp only [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
196	    congr 2
197	
198	/-- **The free-presheaf Čech complex of a finite open cover** (`def:cech_free_presheaf_complex`).
199	
200	The chain complex `K(𝒰)_•` in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is
201	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
202	`= ⨁_σ (PresheafOfModules.free _).obj (yoneda.obj (⨅ k, U (σ k)))`, with the
203	alternating-sum index-dropping differential. Built as the `alternatingFaceMapComplex` of
204	`cechFreeSimplicial`, so `d² = 0` holds by the simplicial identities.
205	
206	Project-local: the free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
207	noncomputable def cechFreePresheafComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
208	    ChainComplex X.PresheafOfModules ℕ :=
209	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex X.PresheafOfModules).obj (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰)
210	
211	/-- Degreewise unfolding of the free Čech complex: the degree-`p` term is the coproduct of
212	free presheaves over the `(p+1)`-fold multi-indices. Exposed (it holds by `rfl`) so that the
213	downstream hom-identification and quasi-isomorphism arguments can rewrite the degree-`p`
214	object without unfolding `alternatingFaceMapComplex` and `cechFreeSimplicial` by hand. -/
215	lemma cechFreePresheafComplex_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
216	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p
217	      = ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) :=
218	  rfl
219	
220	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — augmentation and cover structure presheaf -/
221	
222	/-- **Augmentation of a representable free presheaf onto the structure presheaf.**
223	
224	The canonical map `freeYoneda V ⟶ unit` (= `O_X`) corresponding under the free–Yoneda hom
225	bijection to the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)`. Concretely it sends the free generator over a
226	`W ⊆ V` to `1 ∈ O_X(W)`. This is the per-summand component of the Čech augmentation
227	`K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰`.
228	
229	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `freeYoneda` nor its augmentation. -/
230	noncomputable def freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
231	    freeYoneda.obj V ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
232	  (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).symm
233	    (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)))
234	
235	/-- Value of the free–Yoneda hom bijection on the augmentation: it is the unit section `1`. -/
236	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
237	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj) (freeYonedaAug V)
238	      = (1 : (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))) := by
239	  simp only [freeYonedaAug, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
240	
241	/-- Value of the augmentation `freeYoneda V' ⟶ O_X` on a generator `freeMk g` (`g : V ⟶ V'`):
242	it is the unit section `1 ∈ O_X(V)` (the restriction of `1 ∈ O_X(V')`). -/
243	private lemma freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (g : V ⟶ V') :
244	    (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk g)
245	      = (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) := by
246	  have key := (freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply V' (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
247	      (freeYonedaAug V')).symm.trans (freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug V')
248	  have hmap : (ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYoneda.obj V').map g.op))
249	      (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V')) = ModuleCat.freeMk g := by
250	    erw [PresheafOfModules.freeObj_map, ModuleCat.freeDesc_apply]
251	    change ModuleCat.freeMk ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((yoneda.obj V').map g.op)) (𝟙 V'))
252	      = ModuleCat.freeMk g
253	    congr 1
254	  have hnat := PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply (freeYonedaAug V') g.op
255	    (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))
256	  rw [hmap] at hnat
257	  rw [hnat]
258	  change (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op))
259	    ((ConcreteCategory.hom ((freeYonedaAug V').app (Opposite.op V'))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V'))) = 1
260	  exact (congrArg (ConcreteCategory.hom (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map g.op)) key).trans (map_one _)
261	
262	/-- **Naturality of the augmentation.** For `V ≤ V'` the restriction map of free presheaves
263	composed with the augmentation onto `O_X` equals the augmentation over the smaller open:
264	`freeYoneda.map (incl) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V`. This is the cocone condition
265	that turns the per-summand augmentations into a chain map `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
266	lemma freeYoneda_map_comp_aug {V V' : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ V') :
267	    freeYoneda.map (homOfLE h) ≫ freeYonedaAug V' = freeYonedaAug V := by
268	  apply (freeYonedaHomEquiv V (PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).injective
269	  rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_freeYonedaAug, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, PresheafOfModules.comp_app]
270	  erw [ModuleCat.comp_apply, Functor.comp_map,
271	    PresheafOfModules.free_map_app, ModuleCat.free_map_apply, freeYonedaAug_app_freeMk]
272	
273	/-- **Degree-`0` augmentation of the free Čech complex.** The map
274	`K(𝒰)_0 = ∐_{σ : Fin 1 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X` assembled from the per-summand
275	augmentations `freeYonedaAug`. Its image presheaf is the cover structure presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
276	noncomputable def cechFreeAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
277	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X 0 ⟶ PresheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf.obj :=
278	  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)
279	
280	/-- **The cover structure presheaf** `O_𝒰` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
281	
282	The image (as a presheaf of `O_X`-modules) of the degree-`0` augmentation `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_X`
283	(`cechFreeAug`). Concretely `O_𝒰(W) = O_X(W)` when `W` is contained in some cover member
284	`U_i`, and `0` otherwise. The free Čech complex `K(𝒰)_•` is a resolution of `O_𝒰`
285	(`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`).
286	
287	Project-local: the image presheaf of a cover's augmentation has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
288	noncomputable def coverStructurePresheaf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
289	    X.PresheafOfModules :=
290	  Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)
291	
292	/-- Each face map `δ i : K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation is the same
293	multi-indexed augmentation `∐_{σ : Fin 2 → I} freeYoneda(U_σ) ⟶ O_X`, independent of `i`.
294	This is the cosimplicial-identity input to `d ≫ aug = 0`: the two faces become equal after
295	augmenting, by naturality of `freeYonedaAug`. -/
296	private lemma cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (i : Fin 2) :
297	    (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰
298	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun σ : Fin 2 → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYonedaAug (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
299	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
300	  intro σ
301	  simp only [SimplicialObject.δ, cechFreeSimplicial, cechFreeAug, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
302	    Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
303	  exact freeYoneda_map_comp_aug (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 _ σ)
304	
305	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the augmentation vanishes: the
306	alternating face sum `δ_0 - δ_1` is killed because both faces agree after augmenting
307	(`cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`). This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
308	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]`. -/
309	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_aug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
310	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
311	  have hd : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0
312	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0 :=
313	    AlgebraicTopology.alternatingFaceMapComplex_obj_d (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
314	  -- Prove the simplicial-level identity as a standalone `have` so its composition `≫` is
315	  -- elaborated fresh at the `cechFreeSimplicial.obj` types — matching
316	  -- `cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug`. Rewriting `hd` into the goal first would pin the composition
317	  -- at the `.X` types of the chain complex, which are only definitionally (not syntactically)
318	  -- equal and block the rewrites.
319	  have main : AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) 0
320	      ≫ cechFreeAug 𝒰 = 0 := by
321	    rw [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, Fin.sum_univ_two,
322	      Preadditive.add_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp, Preadditive.zsmul_comp,
323	      cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug, cechFreeSimplicial_δ_comp_aug]
324	    simp only [Fin.isValue, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
325	    abel
326	  rw [hd]; exact main
327	
328	/-- The differential `K(𝒰)_1 ⟶ K(𝒰)_0` composed with the map onto the image presheaf
329	`O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` vanishes. Obtained from `cechFree_d_comp_aug` by cancelling the
330	mono `image.ι`. This is the cochain-map condition for the augmentation
331	`K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`cechFreeComplexAug`). -/
332	private lemma cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
333	    (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d 1 0 ≫ Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) = 0 := by
334	  rw [← cancel_mono (Limits.image.ι (cechFreeAug 𝒰)), Category.assoc, Limits.image.fac,
335	    Limits.zero_comp]
336	  exact cechFree_d_comp_aug 𝒰
337	
338	/-- **The augmentation chain map** `K(𝒰)_• ⟶ O_𝒰[0]` (`def:cover_structure_presheaf`).
339	
340	The chain map from the free Čech complex to the cover structure presheaf concentrated in degree
341	`0`, whose degree-`0` component is the canonical map `K(𝒰)_0 ⟶ O_𝒰 = image(cechFreeAug)` onto the
342	image presheaf. The chain-map condition `d ≫ aug = 0` is `cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage`. The
343	quasi-isomorphism claim `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` asserts this map is a quasi-isomorphism.
344	
345	Project-local: the augmented free Čech resolution of a cover is not in Mathlib. -/
346	noncomputable def cechFreeComplexAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
347	    cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰 ⟶
348	      (ChainComplex.single₀ X.PresheafOfModules).obj (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰) :=
349	  ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).toSingle₀Equiv (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)).symm
350	    ⟨Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰), cechFree_d_comp_factorThruImage 𝒰⟩
351	
352	/-- The degree-`0` component of the augmentation chain map is the canonical map onto the image
353	presheaf `O_𝒰`. -/
354	lemma cechFreeComplexAug_f_zero (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] :
355	    (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).f 0 = Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰) := by
356	  rw [cechFreeComplexAug, ChainComplex.toSingle₀Equiv_symm_apply_f_zero]
357	
358	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms
359	
360	Homology in `PresheafOfModules R` is computed objectwise: the evaluation functors
361	`PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V` are jointly conservative and preserve homology, so a
362	morphism of complexes of presheaves of modules is a quasi-isomorphism as soon as each of its
363	evaluations is.  These three lemmas package that reduction.  Mathlib has the single-functor
364	statement `HomologicalComplex.quasiIso_map_iff_of_preservesHomology` (which needs the functor to
365	reflect isomorphisms — false for a single evaluation), but not the joint-conservativity version
366	needed to reduce a quasi-isomorphism of presheaf-of-module complexes to its sectionwise checks. -/
367	
368	open HomologicalComplex in
369	/-- For a functor `F` preserving homology, if the induced map on the homology of the mapped
370	complexes is an isomorphism, then `F` applied to the homology map is an isomorphism.  This is the
371	naturality square of `ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso` read off at a fixed degree. -/
372	private lemma isIso_Fmap_homologyMap {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {C₁ C₂ : Type*}
373	    [Category C₁] [Category C₂] [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive C₂]
374	    {K L : HomologicalComplex C₁ c} (φ : K ⟶ L) (F : C₁ ⥤ C₂) [F.Additive] [F.PreservesHomology]
375	    (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i] [L.HasHomology i]
376	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj K).HasHomology i]
377	    [((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).obj L).HasHomology i]
378	    (hiso : IsIso (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap ((F.mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i)) :
379	    IsIso (F.map (HomologicalComplex.homologyMap φ i)) := by
380	  have key := ShortComplex.mapHomologyIso_hom_naturality ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ) F
381	  haveI hmid : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
382	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := hiso
383	  change IsIso (F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)))
384	  haveI hcomp : IsIso (ShortComplex.homologyMap
385	      (F.mapShortComplex.map ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ)) ≫
386	      (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj L).mapHomologyIso F).hom) := inferInstance
387	  haveI hcomp2 : IsIso ((((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom ≫
388	      F.map (ShortComplex.homologyMap ((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).map φ))) := key ▸ hcomp
389	  exact IsIso.of_isIso_comp_left
390	    (((shortComplexFunctor C₁ c i).obj K).mapHomologyIso F).hom _
391	
392	/-- **Joint conservativity of evaluations.** A morphism of presheaves of `R`-modules is an
393	isomorphism as soon as each of its evaluations `(evaluation R V).map g` is, since the underlying
394	presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of presheaves is an
395	isomorphism iff it is so objectwise.  Project-local: the joint-conservativity packaging is not in
396	Mathlib. -/
397	private lemma isIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat)
398	    {M N : PresheafOfModules R} (g : M ⟶ N)
399	    (h : ∀ V, IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).map g)) : IsIso g := by
400	  suffices hh : IsIso ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g) from
401	    isIso_of_reflects_iso g (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R)
402	  rw [NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
403	  intro V
404	  have hV := h V
405	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map] at hV
406	  haveI : IsIso (g.app V) := hV
407	  have e : ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf R).map g).app V
408	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab).map (g.app V) := rfl
409	  rw [e]
410	  exact Functor.map_isIso (forget₂ (ModuleCat (R.obj V)) Ab) (g.app V)
411	
412	/-- **Objectwise reduction of quasi-isomorphisms.** A morphism `φ` of chain complexes of
413	presheaves of `R`-modules is a quasi-isomorphism if, for every object `V`, its evaluation
414	`(evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex |>.map φ` (a morphism of complexes of `R(V)`-modules) is a
415	quasi-isomorphism.  This is the formal core of the sectionwise argument that the free Čech complex
416	resolves the cover structure presheaf: homology of presheaves of modules is computed objectwise.
417	
418	Project-local: combines `isIso_of_evaluation` (joint conservativity) with `isIso_Fmap_homologyMap`
419	(evaluation preserves homology). -/
420	lemma quasiIso_of_evaluation {C : Type*} [Category C] (R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat) {ι : Type*}
421	    {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex (PresheafOfModules R) c} (φ : K ⟶ L)
422	    [∀ i, K.HasHomology i] [∀ i, L.HasHomology i]
423	    (h : ∀ V, QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ)) :
424	    QuasiIso φ := by
425	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
426	  intro i
427	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
428	  apply isIso_of_evaluation R
429	  intro V
430	  rw [PresheafOfModules.evaluation_map]
431	  haveI hqV : QuasiIsoAt (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) i :=
432	    (h V).quasiIsoAt i
433	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap] at hqV
434	  exact isIso_Fmap_homologyMap φ (PresheafOfModules.evaluation R V) i hqV
435	
436	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — combinatorial contracting-homotopy engine
437	
438	The objectwise reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` turns `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` into a
439	per-open-`V` statement: the evaluation of the augmented free Čech complex at `V` is the
440	augmented combinatorial Čech complex of the full simplex on `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` with
441	constant coefficients the ring `O_X(V)`.  This section ports the constant-coefficient
442	combinatorial contracting homotopy — the prepend-`i_fix` map and its `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id`
443	identity — into this file as a self-contained algebraic engine.
444	
445	This is the free-side analogue of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy` in `CechAcyclic.lean`
446	(those declarations are `private` there, hence unavailable here); the proofs are the same
447	alternating-sum cancellation, specialised to the constant coefficient module.  Here `M` is
448	the constant coefficient module (to be `O_X(V)` in the application) and `ι` the index type
449	(to be `I₁(V)`). -/
450	
451	namespace FreeCechEngine
452	
453	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
454	
455	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
456	`(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`, the alternating sum of the index-dropping
457	maps.  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential`. -/
458	def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
459	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
460	
461	/-- The contracting homotopy: prepend the fixed index `r`.  `(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`.
462	Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`. -/
463	def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
464	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
465	
466	@[simp] lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
467	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
468	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
469	
470	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(k+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the `k`-th
471	coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
472	lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
473	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
474	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
475	  funext l
476	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
477	  · simp
478	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
479	
480	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** `d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on the constant-coefficient
481	Čech complex (Stacks `lemma-homology-complex`, the `dh + hd = id` computation).  Free-side
482	port of `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy_spec`. -/
483	lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
484	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
485	  funext σ
486	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
487	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
488	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
489	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
490	    funext i; simp
491	  rw [h0]
492	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
493	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
494	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
495	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
496	
497	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then `t = d (h t)`. -/
498	lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
499	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
500	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
501	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
502	  exact h
503	
504	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
505	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
506	lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
507	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
508	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
509	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
510	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
511	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
512	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
513	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
514	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
515	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
516	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
517	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
518	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
519	
520	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex, via the
521	sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)`. -/
522	lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
523	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
524	  funext σ
525	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
526	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
527	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
528	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
529	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
530	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
531	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
532	      funext k
533	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
534	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
535	    simp only [harg]
536	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
537	    simp
538	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
539	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
540	    intro hj
541	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
542	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
543	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
544	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
545	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
546	
547	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex
548	(`Function.Exact` form).  Free-side port of `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential_exact`. -/
549	lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
550	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
551	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
552	  intro x
553	  constructor
554	  · intro hx
555	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
556	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
557	    exact combDifferential_comp y
558	
559	end FreeCechEngine
560	
561	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — sectionwise reduction of the free Čech complex -/
562	
563	/-- **Evaluation preserves the degreewise coproduct of the free Čech complex.**
564	
565	The objectwise-reduction `quasiIso_of_evaluation` requires understanding the evaluation of
566	`cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰` at an open `V`.  Since the degree-`p` term is the coproduct
567	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and the evaluation functor
568	preserves finite colimits (`PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits`,
569	with `Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` finite as `𝒰.I₀` is finite), evaluating commutes with the
570	coproduct:
571	`(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p) ≅ ∐_σ (evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`.
572	This is the degree-`p` entry point for the sectionwise description
573	(blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`); each evaluated summand
574	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
575	`O_X(V)`-module on the (subsingleton) hom-set `V ⟶ W`, i.e. `O_X(V)` if `V ≤ W` and `0`
576	otherwise.
577	
578	Project-local: the assembly of evaluation-preserves-coproduct with the free Čech complex
579	has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
580	noncomputable def cechFreeEval_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
581	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ) (p : ℕ) :
582	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)
583	      ≅ ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
584	          (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V).obj
585	            (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) := by
586	  haveI : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀))
587	      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj V) := by
588	    haveI := PresheafOfModules.Finite.evaluation_preservesFiniteColimits X.ringCatSheaf.obj V
589	    infer_instance
590	  exact Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso _ _
591	
592	/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ⊄ W` gives the zero module.**
593	
594	`(evaluation V).obj (freeYoneda.obj W) = (ModuleCat.free (O_X(V))).obj (V ⟶ W)` is the free
595	`O_X(V)`-module on the hom-set `V ⟶ W`.  When `V ≰ W` that hom-set is empty, so the module
596	is `0`.  This is the per-summand input to the empty case of the sectionwise reduction (and
597	kills the non-`I₁` summands in the nonempty case).
598	
599	Project-local: a degreewise vanishing statement for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
600	lemma freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : ¬ V ≤ W) :
601	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
602	      (freeYoneda.obj W)) := by
603	  haveI : IsEmpty (V ⟶ W) := ⟨fun f => h (leOfHom f)⟩
604	  haveI : Subsingleton ↑((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
605	      (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)) :=
606	    ⟨fun a b => Finsupp.ext (fun x => (IsEmpty.false x).elim)⟩
607	  exact ModuleCat.isZero_of_subsingleton _
608	
609	/-- **Evaluating `freeYoneda W` at an open `V ≤ W` gives `O_X(V)`.**
610	
611	When `V ≤ W` the hom-set `V ⟶ W` is a singleton (`Unique`), so the free `O_X(V)`-module on it
612	is `O_X(V)` itself.  This is the per-summand identification of the surviving (`I₁`) summands in
613	the sectionwise reduction (blueprint `lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise`): together with
614	`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` it gives the description
615	`K(𝒰)_p(V) = ⊕_{σ : V ≤ U_σ} O_X(V)`.
616	
617	Project-local: a degreewise identification for the project's `freeYoneda`. -/
618	noncomputable def freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le {W V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h : V ≤ W) :
619	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj (freeYoneda.obj W)
620	      ≅ ModuleCat.of (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))
621	          (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
622	  haveI : Unique (V ⟶ W) := ⟨⟨homOfLE h⟩, fun _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _⟩
623	  (Finsupp.LinearEquiv.finsuppUnique _ _ (V ⟶ W)).toModuleIso
624	
625	/-- A coproduct of zero objects is a zero object: every coproduct injection out of a zero
626	object is zero, so the identity of the coproduct is zero. -/
627	lemma isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero {C : Type*} [Category C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C]
628	    {β : Type*} (f : β → C) [Limits.HasCoproduct f]
629	    (h : ∀ b, Limits.IsZero (f b)) : Limits.IsZero (∐ f) := by
630	  rw [Limits.IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
631	  apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
632	  intro b
633	  rw [Category.comp_id, Limits.comp_zero]
634	  exact (h b).eq_zero_of_src _
635	
636	/-- **Empty case of the sectionwise reduction (object level).**
637	
638	If no cover member `U_i` contains `V` (i.e. `I₁(V) = ∅`), then the degree-`p` term of the
639	free Čech complex evaluates to the zero module at `V`.  Indeed every multi-index intersection
640	`coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ 0)`, so `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` would force `V` into a
641	cover member, contradiction; hence each summand vanishes by `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`
642	and the coproduct is zero.  This is the object-level input to
643	`cech_free_eval_empty`. -/
644	lemma cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
645	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
646	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
647	      ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p)) := by
648	  refine Limits.IsZero.of_iso ?_ (cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p)
649	  apply isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero
650	  intro σ
651	  apply freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le
652	  intro hle
653	  exact hV (σ 0) (le_trans hle (iInf_le (fun k => coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)) 0))
654	
655	/-- **Empty case — the cover structure presheaf evaluates to zero.**
656	
657	If no cover member contains `V` (`I₁(V) = ∅`), then `O_𝒰(V) = 0`.  The cover structure
658	presheaf is the image of the augmentation `cechFreeAug`, so `O_𝒰` is an epimorphic image of
659	`K(𝒰)_0` via `factorThruImage`; evaluating at `V` (which preserves epimorphisms) gives an
660	epimorphism out of the zero module `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_0)`
661	(`cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty`), so its target is zero. -/
662	lemma coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
663	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
664	    Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
665	      (coverStructurePresheaf 𝒰)) := by
666	  change Limits.IsZero ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
667	      (Limits.image (cechFreeAug 𝒰)))
668	  haveI : Epi ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
669	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰))) :=
670	    Functor.map_epi (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V))
671	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰))
672	  exact Limits.IsZero.of_epi
673	    ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).map
674	      (Limits.factorThruImage (cechFreeAug 𝒰)))
675	    (cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V 0 hV)
676	
677	/-- The homology of a homological complex vanishes in any degree where the complex object
678	vanishes: the degree-`i` homology is a subquotient of `K.X i`. -/
679	lemma isZero_homology_of_isZero_X {C : Type*} [Category C] [Limits.HasZeroMorphisms C]
680	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} (K : HomologicalComplex C c) (i : ι) [K.HasHomology i]
681	    (h : Limits.IsZero (K.X i)) : Limits.IsZero (K.homology i) := by
682	  rw [← K.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology i, HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,
683	    ShortComplex.exact_iff_isZero_homology]
684	  exact ShortComplex.isZero_homology_of_isZero_X₂ _ h
685	
686	/-- **Empty case of the sectionwise reduction** (`lem:cech_free_eval_empty`).
687	
688	If no cover member contains `V` (`I₁(V) = ∅`), the evaluation at `V` of the augmentation
689	chain map `cechFreeComplexAug` is a quasi-isomorphism: both the evaluated free complex
690	(`cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty`) and the evaluated target
691	(`coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty`) are objectwise zero, so all their homology
692	vanishes and the induced homology map is an isomorphism between zero objects. -/
693	lemma cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
694	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (hV : ∀ i, ¬ V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i) :
695	    QuasiIso (((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj
696	      (Opposite.op V)).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.down ℕ)).map
697	        (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)) := by
698	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
699	  intro i
700	  rw [quasiIsoAt_iff_isIso_homologyMap]
701	  refine Limits.isIso_of_source_target_iso_zero _
702	    (Limits.IsZero.isoZero ?_) (Limits.IsZero.isoZero ?_)
703	  · exact isZero_homology_of_isZero_X _ i (cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V i hV)
704	  · apply isZero_homology_of_isZero_X
705	    rcases i with _ | n
706	    · exact coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty 𝒰 V hV
707	    · exact Limits.IsZero.of_iso (Limits.isZero_zero _)
708	        ((PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).mapZeroObject)
709	
710	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — degreewise engine model of the evaluated free complex
711	
712	The nonempty-case analysis of the evaluated free Čech complex needs an explicit degreewise
713	identification of `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p)` with the constant-coefficient combinatorial model
714	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`, where `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  This section builds that
715	degreewise object isomorphism as the composite
716	
717	  `(eval V)(K_p) ≅[cechFreeEval_X] ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1)→I₀} (eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)`
718	  `           ≅[drop-zeros]      ∐_{σ : V ≤ U_σ} (eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)`
719	  `           ≅[whiskerEquiv]    ∐_{τ : Fin(p+1)→I₁(V)} O_X(V),`
720	
721	using `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` to discard the summands with `V ≰ U_σ` and
722	`freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` to identify each surviving summand with `O_X(V)`.  The differential
723	match turning this degreewise iso into an iso of chain complexes is the genuine remaining
724	bottleneck and is NOT discharged here. -/
725	
726	/-- `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` holds iff every value of `σ` indexes a cover member containing `V`;
727	i.e. `σ` factors through `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  This is the index-splitting criterion separating
728	the surviving summands of the evaluated free Čech complex from the vanishing ones. -/
729	lemma le_coverInterOpen_iff (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
730	    {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
731	    V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ↔ ∀ k, V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) := by
732	  simp only [coverInterOpen, le_iInf_iff]
733	
734	/-- **Reindexing the surviving multi-indices.** The multi-indices `σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` with
735	`V ≤ U_σ` are in bijection with the maps `Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)` into the subtype
736	`I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}` of cover members containing `V`.  This bijection feeds
737	`Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv` to collapse the
738	surviving coproduct onto the constant-coefficient combinatorial index `Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`. -/
739	def survivingEquiv (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
740	    (Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) ≃
741	      {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} where
742	  toFun τ := ⟨fun k => (τ k).1, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V _).2 fun k => (τ k).2⟩
743	  invFun σ := fun k => ⟨σ.1 k, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V σ.1).1 σ.2 k⟩
744	  left_inv τ := by funext k; rfl
745	  right_inv σ := by ext k; rfl
746	
747	/-- **Drop-zeros isomorphism.** Evaluating the degree-`p` free Čech term at `V` and splitting off
748	the vanishing summands: the full coproduct over all `σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` is isomorphic to the
749	coproduct over the *surviving* multi-indices `{σ : V ≤ U_σ}`, because every non-surviving summand
750	`(eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)` (with `V ≰ U_σ`) is a zero object
751	(`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`).  This is the first of the two halves identifying the evaluated
752	term with the combinatorial model. -/
753	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalDropZeros (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
754	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
755	    (∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
756	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
757	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) ≅
758	      (∐ fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
759	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
760	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1))) where
761	  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ => by
762	    classical
763	    exact if h : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ then
764	      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
765	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
766	          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1))) ⟨σ, h⟩
767	    else 0
768	  inv := Limits.Sigma.desc fun s =>
769	    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
770	      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
771	        (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) s.1
772	  hom_inv_id := by
773	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
774	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id]
775	    by_cases h : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ
776	    · rw [dif_pos h, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
777	    · rw [dif_neg h, Limits.zero_comp]
778	      exact ((freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le h).eq_zero_of_src _).symm
779	  inv_hom_id := by
780	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro s
781	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, dif_pos s.2]
782	
783	/-- **Degreewise engine model of the evaluated free Čech term.** The degree-`p` term of the
784	evaluated free Čech complex at `V` is isomorphic to the constant-coefficient combinatorial model
785	`∐_{τ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`, where `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  Built as the composite of
786	`cechFreeEval_X` (evaluation commutes with the coproduct), `cechFreeEvalDropZeros` (discard the
787	`V ≰ U_σ` summands), and `Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv` along `survivingEquiv` with the per-summand
788	identification `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`.  This is the object half of the engine iso
789	`cechFreeEvalEngineIso`; the differential match remains. -/
790	noncomputable def cechFreeEvalEngine_X (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
791	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
792	    (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
793	        ((cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).X p) ≅
794	      ∐ fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} =>
795	        ModuleCat.of (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))
796	          (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
797	  cechFreeEval_X 𝒰 (Opposite.op V) p ≪≫ cechFreeEvalDropZeros 𝒰 V p ≪≫
798	    (Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv (survivingEquiv 𝒰 V p)
799	      (g := fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
800	        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj

1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean
5	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PresheafCech.lean
6	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FreePresheafComplex.lean
7	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechBridge.lean
8	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean
9	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
10	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
11	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
12	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
13	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
14	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}) (read from
15	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
16	
17	\section{Setup and motivation}
18	
19	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
20	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
21	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
22	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
23	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
24	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
25	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
26	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
27	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
28	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
29	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
30	
31	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
32	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
33	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
34	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
35	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
36	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
37	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
38	\[
39	  \begin{array}{ccc}
40	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
41	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
42	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
43	  \end{array}
44	\]
45	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
46	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
47	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
48	
49	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
50	
51	\begin{definition}
52	
53	\leanok
54	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
55	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
56	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
57	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor, def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
58	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
59	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
60	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
61	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
62	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
63	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
64	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
65	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
66	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
67	  % the unit ideal, see
68	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
69	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
70	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
71	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
72	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
73	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
74	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
75	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
76	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
77	  \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in
78	  cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
79	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
80	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
81	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
82	  \[
83	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
84	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
85	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
86	  \]
87	  with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and
88	  codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree
89	  \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric

95	  raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection

2427	\begin{lemma}[Right adjoint of a mono-preserving left adjoint preserves injectives]
2428	  \label{lem:injective_of_adjoint}

2433	  Let \(L \dashv R\) be an adjunction between abelian (or, more generally, suitable)
2434	  categories such that the left adjoint \(L\) preserves monomorphisms. Then for every
2435	  injective object \(J\) of the target, \(R(J)\) is an injective object of the source.

1345	\subsection{Presheaf-level {\v C}ech machinery}
1346	\label{sec:presheaf_cech_machinery}
1347	
1348	The {\v C}ech-acyclicity of injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
1349	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} below) is not a statement about a single
1350	affine cover: it is proved on the abelian category \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
1351	of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, where the {\v C}ech complex of sections
1352	is the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex of an explicit free resolution. The argument
1353	rests on two presheaf-level facts, recorded here, and one transfer of injectivity.
1354	First, a free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of presheaves of modules whose
1355	\(\operatorname{Hom}\)-complex into \(\mathcal{F}\) \emph{is} the {\v C}ech complex
1356	of sections (Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex},
1357	Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and
1358	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification}). Second, the resolution property of
1359	that complex: \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) resolves \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\)
1360	concentrated in degree \(0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso}). Together
1361	these give: an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module is injective as a presheaf
1362	(Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic} via
1363	Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} applied to the sheafification adjunction
1364	Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), so \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) is
1365	exact and carries the augmented resolution to an exact section complex, i.e.\
1366	\(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for \(p > 0\). This direct route uses
1367	neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
1368	\(\delta\)-functor identification.
1369	
1370	\begin{definition}
1371	
1372	[Free presheaf complex of a cover]
1373	  \label{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1374	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex,
1375	    AlgebraicGeometry.freeYoneda,
1376	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverOpen,
1377	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverInterOpen,
1378	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverInterOpen_comp_le,
1379	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeSimplicial,
1380	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreePresheafComplex_X,
1381	    AlgebraicGeometry.sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport}
1382	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into}
1383	  % (the complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$) (read from
1384	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1142--1162, with the $j_{p!}$ description
1385	  % L1095--1135).
1386	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider the chain complex $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$
1387	  % of presheaves of $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules
1388	  % $$
1389	  % \ldots
1390	  % \to
1391	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1i_2} (j_{i_0i_1i_2})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1i_2}}
1392	  % \to
1393	  % \bigoplus_{i_0i_1} (j_{i_0i_1})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0i_1}}
1394	  % \to
1395	  % \bigoplus_{i_0} (j_{i_0})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0}}
1396	  % \to 0 \to \ldots
1397	  % $$
1398	  % where the last nonzero term is placed in degree $0$
1399	  % and where the map
1400	  % $$
1401	  % (j_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots i_{p + 1}}}
1402	  % \longrightarrow
1403	  % (j_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}})_{p!}
1404	  % \mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0\ldots \hat i_j \ldots i_{p + 1}}}
1405	  % $$
1406	  % is given by $(-1)^j$ times the canonical map."
1407	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
1408	  \texttt{lemma-cech-map-into} (the free complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\)).}
1409	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
1410	  open covering, with \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\)
1411	  the open immersion of each finite intersection. The Stacks formulation uses, for
1412	  each intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), the \emph{extension-by-zero} presheaf
1413	  \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\), the presheaf of
1414	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
1415	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
1416	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
1417	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
1418	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
1419	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
1420	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
1421	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
1422	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
1423	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
1424	  \[
1425	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
1426	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1427	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
1428	  \]
1429	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
1430	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
1431	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
1432	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
1433	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
1434	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
1435	  \[
1436	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
1437	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1438	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1439	  \]
1440	  with \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) placed in degree \(p \geq 0\) (and \(0\) in negative
1441	  degrees), and whose differential \(K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1} \to K(\mathcal{U})_p\) is
1442	  the alternating sum \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\) of the maps
1443	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to
1444	  \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}})\)
1445	  obtained by applying \(\operatorname{free}\) to the representable index-dropping
1446	  maps \(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \to \mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1447	  \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\) induced by the inclusions of opens
1448	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \subset U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\)
1449	  that drop the index \(i_j\). Each summand is a \emph{projective} object of
1450	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\), because the universal property above identifies
1451	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
1452	  i_p}), -)\) with the exact ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1453	  \((-)(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\); this is the structural feature exploited below.
1454	
1455	  We assume the index set \(I\) is \emph{finite} (so the required coproducts exist in
1456	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of the
1457	  comparison target). Here the direct sum \(\bigoplus\) is the categorical coproduct
1458	  \(\coprod\) (the indexed \(\operatorname{Sigma}\) colimit), which for a finite index
1459	  set coincides with the finite biproduct.
1460	\end{definition}

1462	\begin{definition}
1463

1495	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open
1496	  covering. For a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\mathcal{F}\), the
1497	  \emph{section {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
1498	  \mathcal{F})\) is the cochain complex of \emph{abelian groups} whose term in
1499	  degree \(p\) is the product of sections over the \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
1500	  \[
1501	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
1502	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
1503	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
1504	  \]
1505	  each factor being the value of the ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
1506	  (the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)), with
1507	  differential the alternating sum of restriction maps
1508	  \[
1509	    d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1510	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
1511	        s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}
1512	        \big|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}.
1513	  \]
1514	  This object is \emph{distinct} from the relative {\v C}ech complex
1515	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
1516	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}: the latter is the pushforward complex with terms
1517	  \(\prod (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\) in
1518	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\), an object of relative sheaves over the base, whereas the
1519	  section {\v C}ech complex here is the simpler complex of \emph{global sections} of
1520	  \(\mathcal{F}\) over the intersections, an object of abelian groups (the underlying
1521	  additive structure of the sections). The two must not be conflated: the
1522	  presheaf-level acyclicity argument below runs entirely on this section complex.
1523	\end{definition}

1586	% $$

1591	\begin{proof}
1592	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:section_cech_complex}
1593	  The free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules gives, composed with the
1594	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, \mathcal{F}) \cong
1595	  \mathcal{F}(V)\), the natural identification, for each multi-index,
1596	  \[
1597	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}\bigl(
1598	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),\, \mathcal{F}
1599	    \bigr)
1600	    \;\cong\;
1601	    \operatorname{Hom}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p},\,
1602	      \mathcal{F}\bigr)
1603	    \;\cong\;
1604	    \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}),
1605	  \]
1606	  where the last group is the evaluation of \(\mathcal{F}\) at the open
1607	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). Taking the product over multi-indices,
1608	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_p, \mathcal{F}) =
1609	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}) =
1610	  \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\); the direct sum
1611	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) therefore represents the functor \(\mathcal{F} \mapsto
1612	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\). Under this
1613	  identification the \((-1)^j\) maps \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0
1614	  \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j}
1615	  \ldots i_{p+1}})\) defining the differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) become
1616	  exactly the alternating-sum restriction differential of
1617	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so the two cochain
1618	  complexes coincide; naturality in \(\mathcal{F}\) is immediate from the
1619	  naturality of the free–forgetful adjunction and of Yoneda. The per-multi-index
1620	  bijection \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,
1621	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}), \mathcal{F}) \cong \mathcal{F}(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\) is additive,
1622	  so it is promoted to an isomorphism of abelian groups (an \emph{additive}
1623	  equivalence); taking the product over multi-indices and assembling these degreewise
1624	  additive isomorphisms — which commute with the alternating-sum differentials — yields
1625	  the complex isomorphism by matching components degree by degree.
1626	\end{proof}

1669	\begin{lemma}
1670	[Objectwise detection of quasi-isomorphisms of presheaf-of-module complexes]
1671	  \label{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation}
1672	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.quasiIso_of_evaluation,
1673	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_Fmap_homologyMap,
1674	    AlgebraicGeometry.isIso_of_evaluation}
1675	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1676	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1677	  % (proof, the sectionwise-reduction step) (read from
1678	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1220--1226).
1679	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended
1680	  % complex over any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex."
1681	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1682	  (sectionwise reduction).}
1683	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of rings on a category \(C\) and let
1684	  \(\varphi : K \to L\) be a morphism of chain complexes in the abelian category
1685	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. Suppose that for \emph{every}
1686	  object \(V\) the evaluated morphism — the image of \(\varphi\) under the functor
1687	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\bigr).
1688	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\), a morphism of complexes of
1689	  \(R(V)\)-modules — is a quasi-isomorphism. Then \(\varphi\) is a
1690	  quasi-isomorphism. This is the objectwise-reduction step that turns the
1691	  resolution claim of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} into a family of
1692	  sectionwise (combinatorial) statements: homology in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is
1693	  computed open-by-open, because the evaluation functors
1694	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) are exact (hence preserve
1695	  homology) and jointly conservative. The Lean entry point is
1696	  \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\), which combines the joint
1697	  conservativity of the evaluations (each \(g\) is an isomorphism as soon as every
1698	  \((\operatorname{evaluation} R\, V).\operatorname{map} g\) is) with the fact that
1699	  each \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) preserves homology.
1700	\end{lemma}
1701	
1702	\begin{proof}
1703	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1704	  A morphism of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism iff it induces an isomorphism on
1705	  homology in every degree (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\),
1706	  \(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_iff\_isIso\_homologyMap}\)). Fix a degree \(i\). The
1707	  homology map \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) iff each of
1708	  its evaluations is an isomorphism, since the underlying
1709	  presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of
1710	  presheaves is an isomorphism iff it is so objectwise (joint conservativity). For
1711	  each \(V\), evaluation preserves homology, so the evaluation of \(H_i(\varphi)\)
1712	  is the homology map of the evaluated complex morphism; the latter is an
1713	  isomorphism because the evaluated morphism is a quasi-isomorphism by hypothesis.
1714	  Hence \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism for all \(i\), i.e.\ \(\varphi\) is a
1715	  quasi-isomorphism.
1716	\end{proof}

1779	\begin{proof}
1780	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1781	  The degreewise identity is the value at \(V\) of the explicit description of the
1782	  free presheaf \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)\) recorded in
1783	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}: it sends \(V\) to the free
1784	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module on \(\operatorname{Hom}(V, W)\), which is
1785	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) if \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise. Applying
1786	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\;V\) to the coproduct decomposition
1787	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) commutes with the direct sum and
1788	  collapses each summand to \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) or \(0\) according to whether
1789	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\), i.e.\ whether \(\sigma\) factors through \(I_1\); the surviving
1790	  summands are exactly those indexed by \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The
1791	  evaluated faces are the index-dropping restriction maps, which on the surviving
1792	  summands are identities of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), so the alternating sum is the
1793	  combinatorial {\v C}ech differential. The augmentation value follows from
1794	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) exactly when some \(U_i\)
1795	  contains \(V\) (\(I_1 \neq \varnothing\)), recorded in
1796	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}.
1797	\end{proof}

1803	\begin{lemma}
1804	[Constant-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech contracting-homotopy engine]
1805	  \label{lem:free_cech_engine}
1806	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential,
1807	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_comp,
1808	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle,
1809	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_exact,
1810	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy,
1811	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_spec,
1812	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_zero,
1813	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combSign_flip,
1814	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.cons_comp_succAbove_succ,
1815	    AlgebraicGeometry.isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero}
1816	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1817	  % (proof, the differential and contracting map of $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$) (read from
1818	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1245--1268).
1819	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Moreover, the differential of the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$
1820	  % is given by
1821	  % $$
1822	  % d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} =
1823	  % \sum\nolimits_{j = 0, \ldots, p + 1} \sum\nolimits_{i \in I_1}
1824	  % (-1)^j s_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}.
1825	  % $$
1826	  % The sum is finite as the element $s$ has finite support.
1827	  % Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
1828	  % $$
1829	  % h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)
1830	  % $$
1831	  % by the rule
1832	  % $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} =
1833	  % \left\{ \begin{matrix}
1834	  % 0 & \text{if} & i_0 \not = i_{\text{fix}} \\
1835	  % s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p + 1}} & \text{if} & i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}
1836	  % \end{matrix} \right.$"
1837	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1838	  (differential and contracting homotopy of the evaluated complex).}
1839	  Fix a nonempty index set \(J\) (in the application \(J = I_1(V)\)), a chosen index
1840	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in J\), and a single coefficient module \(M\). The
1841	  \emph{constant-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech engine} consists of: the
1842	  alternating-sum {\v C}ech differential \(\operatorname{combDifferential}\) on the
1843	  full simplex over \(J\) with coefficients \(M\),
1844	  \[
1845	    (\operatorname{combDifferential} t)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1846	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\, t_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}},
1847	  \]
1848	  which squares to zero, \(\operatorname{combDifferential} \circ
1849	  \operatorname{combDifferential} = 0\) (\(\operatorname{combDifferential\_comp}\)); the
1850	  \emph{prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)} homotopy \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\),
1851	  \((\operatorname{combHomotopy}\, i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, u)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1852	  = u_{i_1 \ldots i_{p+1}}\) when \(i_0 = i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) and \(0\) otherwise; and
1853	  the contracting identity \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\)
1854	  (\(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_spec}\)), which yields positive-degree exactness
1855	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_exact}\) and the cocycle-splitting form
1856	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_eq\_of\_cocycle}\). The remaining declarations are
1857	  sign- and index-bookkeeping consumed by the contracting identity: the
1858	  succ-shift reindexing \(\operatorname{cons\_comp\_succAbove\_succ}\) and the
1859	  alternating-sign flip \(\operatorname{combSign\_flip}\), together with the
1860	  degenerate-case simplification \(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_zero}\). The generic
1861	  category-theory helper \(\operatorname{isZero\_sigma\_of\_forall\_isZero}\) (a
1862	  coproduct of zero objects is a zero object) is recorded here as well, used to collapse
1863	  the empty-index direct sums of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}. This engine is
1864	  self-contained combinatorics on \(\operatorname{Fin.cons}\),
1865	  \(\operatorname{Fin.succAbove}\), \(\operatorname{Fin.predAbove}\), and
1866	  \(\operatorname{Finset.sum\_involution}\); it mirrors the contracting homotopy of the
1867	  Stacks \texttt{lemma-homology-complex} proof.
1868	\end{lemma}

1870	\begin{lemma}
1871	[The constant-coefficient {\v C}ech engine \emph{chain} complex \(C_\bullet\)]
1872	  \label{lem:cech_engine_complex}
1873	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.le_coverInterOpen_iff,
1874	    AlgebraicGeometry.survivingEquiv,
1875	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalDropZeros,

1886	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechEngineD_exact}
1887	  \uses{lem:free_cech_engine}
1888	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1889	  % (proof, the description of $K^{ext}_p(W)$ and its differential) (read from
1890	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1236--1251).
1891	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1892	  % $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$
1893	  % and
1894	  % $$
1895	  % K^{ext}_p(W) =
1896	  % \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W).
1897	  % $$
1898	  % This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
1899	  % $(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$.
1900	  % Moreover, the differential of the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$
1901	  % is given by
1902	  % $$
1903	  % d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} =
1904	  % \sum\nolimits_{j = 0, \ldots, p + 1} \sum\nolimits_{i \in I_1}
1905	  % (-1)^j s_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}.
1906	  % $$"
1907	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1908	  (the evaluated complex \(K^{ext}_\bullet(W)\) and its differential).}
1909	  Fix an open \(V\) with \(I_1(V) = \{\, i : V \leq U_i \,\} \neq \varnothing\), and
1910	  let \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) be the single coefficient module
1911	  (\(\operatorname{coverSectionModule}\)). The \emph{engine chain complex}
1912	  \(C_\bullet = \operatorname{cechEngineComplex}\, \mathcal{U}\, V\) is the chain
1913	  complex of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-modules with
1914	  \[
1915	    C_p \;=\; \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)} \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1916	    \qquad (\operatorname{cechEngineX}),
1917	  \]
1918	  the coproduct taken in \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(\mathcal{O}_X(V))\), and with
1919	  differential \(d = \operatorname{cechEngineD}\) given on the injection
1920	  \(\iota_\sigma\) of the index \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)\) by the
1921	  alternating sum of index-drop reindexings,
1922	  \[
1923	    d \circ \iota_\sigma
1924	      \;=\; \sum_{i=0}^{p} (-1)^i\, \iota_{\sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i}
1925	    \qquad (\operatorname{cechEngineD\_\iota}),
1926	  \]
1927	  i.e.\ the chain transpose of the index-\emph{raise} differential
1928	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\) of
1929	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}. Then:
1930	  \begin{itemize}
1931	    \item \(d \circ d = 0\) (\(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_comp}\)), so the family
1932	      assembles into the chain complex \(C_\bullet = \operatorname{ChainComplex.of}\)
1933	      (\(\operatorname{cechEngineComplex}\)).
1934	    \item The \emph{prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)} map
1935	      \(s \mapsto \iota_{\operatorname{Fin.cons}\, i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, \sigma}\)
1936	      (\(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend}\), with its injection computation
1937	      \(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend\_\iota}\)) is a contracting homotopy in
1938	      positive degree: \(s \circ d + d \circ s = \mathrm{id}\)
1939	      (\(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend\_spec}\)).
1940	    \item Hence \(C_\bullet\) is exact in positive degree,
1941	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(\operatorname{cechEngineD}(n+1),
1942	      \operatorname{cechEngineD} n)\) (\(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_exact}\)).
1943	  \end{itemize}
1944	  Four object-half bricks realise the degreewise identification
1945	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong C_p\) consumed by
1946	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}: evaluation commutes with the coproduct;
1947	  the non-surviving summands (those \(\sigma\) with \(V \not\leq U_\sigma\)) drop to the
1948	  zero object (\(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\),
1949	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalEngine\_X}\)); and the surviving index type is reindexed
1950	  by the equivalence
1951	  \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv} : (\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)) \simeq
1952	  \{\, \sigma \;//\; V \leq \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\, \mathcal{U}\, \sigma \,\}\),
1953	  where the membership criterion
1954	  \(V \leq \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\, \mathcal{U}\, \sigma
1955	  \iff \forall k,\ V \leq U_{\sigma(k)}\) is
1956	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\).
1957	\end{lemma}
1958	
1959	\begin{proof}
1960	  \uses{lem:free_cech_engine}
1961	  The complex \(C_\bullet\) is the chain/coproduct dual of the constant-coefficient
1962	  core of Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}. The differential
1963	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD}\) is defined on coproduct injections by the
1964	  alternating-sign index-drop \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove}
1965	  i\); both \(d \circ d = 0\) and the contracting identity
1966	  \(s \circ d + d \circ s = \mathrm{id}\) are proved by extending along the
1967	  injections with \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\) and reducing each
1968	  injection-component to the corresponding combinatorial cancellation of
1969	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential\_comp}\) respectively
1970	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\) (the prepend
1971	  \(\operatorname{cechEnginePrepend}\) is the coproduct dual of
1972	  \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\)). Positive-degree exactness
1973	  \(\operatorname{cechEngineD\_exact}\) reads off the contracting identity. The four
1974	  object-half bricks are immediate: evaluation preserves \(\coprod\) and the surviving
1975	  summands are picked out by \(\operatorname{survivingEquiv}\) under the criterion
1976	  \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\), the rest being zero objects
1977	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeEvalDropZeros}\)).
1978	\end{proof}
